zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord William Lovell Holland VII於大約1397年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾鐵其馬緒時，他的父親Sir John Lovel, KN, KG58歲，母親Eleanor la Zouche38歲。他於1420年之前與Alice Deincourt在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1455年6月13日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾明斯特 洛弗爾享年59歲。

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howland
meanings: History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Lovell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated in the Middle Ages from the Old (Norman) French nickname Louvel ‘wolf-cub’, a diminutive of lou ‘wolf’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord William Lovell Holland VII was born about 1397, in Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Lovel, KN, KG, was 58 and his mother, Eleanor la Zouche, was 38. He married Alice Deincourt before 1420, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 13 June 1455, in Minster Lovell, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord William Lovell Holland VII est né environ 1397, à Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Lovel, KN, KG, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Eleanor la Zouche, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Alice Deincourt avant 1420, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 13 juin 1455, à Minster Lovell, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans.

Er hat vor 1420 in Europa Alice Deincourt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 13. ja.lifeSummary: Lord William Lovell Holland VIIが約1397年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアティトチマーッシュで生まれた時，父Sir John Lovel, KN, KGは58歳，母Eleanor la Zoucheは38歳であった。1420年以前にヨーロッパでAlice Deincourtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1455年6月13日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアミンスター ロベルで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord William Lovell Holland VII(이)가 대략 1397년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 티트ch마시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Lovel, KN, KG(은)는 58세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor la Zouche(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1420년 이전 우럽에서 Alice Deincourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1455년 6월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 민스터 러벨에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord William Lovell Holland VII родился около 1397 г. в местности тичмарш, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Lovel, KN, KG было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor la Zouche, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Deincourt перед 1420 г. в местности Европа. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 13 июня 1455 г. в местности минстир лавелл, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет.
